_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Proceed with Uretero-vaginal / Uterine fistula repair only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation:

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP)/ CT-IVP/CT-urogram report + cystoscopy and vaginoscopy report

ppd_preauth.q: Was Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management submitted?
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP)/ CT-IVP/CT-urogram report + cystoscopy and vaginoscopy report submitted?
Did imaging suggest presence of fistula?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Was the Detailed Procedure/Operative notes submitted?
Is the Intra procedure still photograph submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing, incomplete, or not detailing evaluation findings, indication, or planned management.
Imaging (IVP/CT-IVP/CT-urogram) or endoscopic (cystoscopy/vaginoscopy) reports missing or incomplete, preventing confirmation of fistula diagnosis.
Intra-procedure still photograph not submitted at claim stage.
Imaging reports (IVP/CT-IVP/CT-urogram) did not confirm the presence of a fistula, or report is unclear.
Operative notes missing, incomplete, or lacking details of surgical steps and findings.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary care and Laparoscopic surgery facility availability.
clinical_key_pointers: A vaginal fistula is an abnormal opening that connects your vagina to another organ, such as your bladder, colon or rectum.
There are several types of vaginal fistulas:
Vesicovaginal fistula: Also called a bladder fistula, this opening occurs between your vagina and urinary bladder and is the type that doctors see most often.
Ureterovaginal fistula: This type of fistula happens when the abnormal opening develops between your vagina and the ducts that carry urine from your kidneys to your bladder (ureters).
Urethrovaginal fistula: In this type of fistula, also called a urethral fistula, the opening occurs between your vagina and the tube that carries urine out of your body (urethra).
Rectovaginal fistula: In this type of fistula, the opening is between your vagina and the lower portion of your large intestine (rectum).
Colovaginal fistula: With a colovaginal fistula, the opening occurs between the vagina and colon.
Enterovaginal fistula: In this type of fistula, the opening is between the small intestine and the vagina.
The evidence relating to diagnosis and treatment of urinary fistula is generally poor and this review inevitably relies largely on numerous case series and other consensus statements.
